name: Summer Circus Spectacular
venue: Historic Asolo Theater at The Ringling
hours_label: Tue–Fri 11 AM & 2 PM, Sat 2 PM & 5 PM · $20 adults / $15 kids
blurb: An intimate, family-friendly one-hour theater circus in the historic Asolo Theater, featuring world-class acrobats, comedy and rotating specialty acts. Tickets are $20 for adults and $15 for children 15 and under, with add-on access to the Circus Museum for $5. A perfect summer outing for families.
photo_credit: Photo: The Ringling / Circus Arts Conservatory

name: Marvin: A Marvin Gaye Revue
hours_label: 7:30 PM Tue–Sat; 2 PM Sat–Sun · $54 adults / $24 students & military
blurb: Nate Jacobs' newly created revue celebrates one of the greatest catalogs in music history, with a live ensemble delivering "How Sweet It Is," "Can I Get a Witness," "Sexual Healing" and "Heard It Through the Grapevine." Tickets are $54 for adults and $24 for students and active military. Closing Jul 24 before the company heads to an international festival.
photo_credit: Photo: Westcoast Black Theatre Troupe / VisitSarasota.com

name: Sarasota Farmers Market
address: Lemon Ave & Main St, Sarasota, FL 34236
blurb: A downtown Saturday tradition spanning Lemon Avenue and Main Street with fresh local produce, flowers, baked goods, artisan foods and live music. Free to browse and family- and dog-friendly. A great way to sample Sarasota's local flavor.

name: Siesta Key Drum Circle
blurb: A beloved weekly gathering on the quartz-white sands of Siesta Beach where drummers, dancers and onlookers welcome the sunset. It kicks off about an hour before sundown just south of the main pavilion. Free and open to all – bring a chair and join the circle.
